system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          0.0025;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|omega|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|k|omega|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    2;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    generalizedNewtonian;

    generalizedNewtonianCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  CrossPowerLaw;

        nuInf   10;
        m       0.4;
        n       3;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

viscosityModel  constant;

nu              1e-02;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;
    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (1.5 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    cylinder
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    defaultFaces
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 2 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    cylinder
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    defaultFaces
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Arc Point Lies on Edge Line

**Error Message:**
```
Arc point (0.33 -0.67 -0.5) lies on the line of edge (16 17)
```

**Diagnosis:**

The issue is with the **arc edge definitions**. The vertices used as endpoints of arcs are also specified as the centers of those same arcs, which causes a geometric inconsistency. For an arc edge, the `center` parameter must define the circle center, and the two vertex indices must lie on the circumference of that circle — not coincident with the center.

Looking at the problematic edges:

- **Edge 16→17**: Vertex 16 = `(0.33 -0.67 -0.5)` is listed as the center, but it's also endpoint 16. This means the arc point coincides with the center → invalid.
- Similar issues exist across multiple arc definitions where the center equals one of the arc endpoints.

**Resolution:**

For each arc edge, the `center` should be a separate point from the two vertex endpoints. Based on the geometry of the offset cylinder, the correct approach is:

1. **Remove the `center` entry entirely** from each arc definition — OpenFOAM can compute the center automatically from the arc specification if only `radius`, `startAngles`, and `endAngles` are provided.
2. Alternatively, **correct the coordinates** so that the center is distinct from the endpoints. For example:
   - For the lower-left arc between vertices 16 and 17, the center should be at approximately `(0.33 + 0.6667, -0.67, -0.5)` ≈ `(1.0, -0.67, -0.5)`, but this needs careful geometric verification.
   - Actually, looking more carefully at the vertex layout, the arcs form a rectangular cylinder shape. Each arc connects adjacent vertices around the intended cylinder cross-section. The center for the arc from 16→17 should be positioned such that both points are equidistant from it. For instance, if the arc represents the bottom face of the cylinder, the center could be at `(0.33, -0.67 - 0.6667, -0.5)` or similar.

However, the simplest and most reliable fix is to **remove the `center` keyword** from all arc definitions and let OpenFOAM calculate it automatically based on the radius and the two endpoints. The arc entries would become:

```
arc 16 17 (0.33 -0.67 -0.5)
    radius 0.666666666666667
    startAngles (0 -180)
    endAngles (0 -0);
```

Repeat this for all 12 arc definitions. Without the explicit `center` parameter, OpenFOAM derives it from the geometry of the two endpoints and the radius, avoiding the conflict.

---

## Error 2: `log.pimpleFoam` — Cannot Find Mesh Files

**Error Message:**
```
Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh"
```

**Diagnosis:**

This is a cascading error. Since `blockMesh` failed (due to the arc edge issue above), no mesh was generated. Therefore, when `pimpleFoam` tries to run, it cannot find the mesh files (`points`, `faces`, etc.) in `constant/polyMesh`.

**Resolution:**

Once the `blockMeshDict` is fixed and `blockMesh` runs successfully to generate the mesh, `pimpleFoam` will proceed normally with the PIMPLE algorithm using the specified parameters (`deltaT = 0.0025`, `endTime = 0.5`, `writeInterval = 0.05`).

---

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **In `system/blockMeshDict`:** Remove the `center` keyword from all 12 `arc` edge definitions. Let OpenFOAM compute the arc centers automatically. Verify the resulting geometry visually if possible.

2. **After fixing `blockMeshDict`:** Re-run `blockMesh` first, then run `pimpleFoam`. The mesh generation must succeed before the CFD simulation can begin.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Remove the center keyword from all 12 arc edge definitions so OpenFOAM computes the arc centers automatically based on the two endpoints and radius'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a laminar offsetcylinder simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm with a generalized Newtonian model and Cross-Power-Law viscosity for momentum transport, characterized by specific parameters nuInf=10, m=0.4, n=3. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 1.5m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls, including the cylinder surface. Use timestep of 0.0025 and output every 0.05. Finaltime is 0.5. use constant viscosity model with nu value of 1e-02 in physical properties.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
